diagrams.title: Procedural vs OOP Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing differences in code organization between procedural and object-oriented approaches
elements: Procedural: Data -> Functions <- OOP: Data + Functions -> Objects -> Classes
Procedural: Sequential execution, global data; OOP: Messages between objects, encapsulated data
Procedural: Top-down design; OOP: Bottom-up design

diagrams.title: OOP Pillars Relationship
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how the four OOP principles relate to each other
elements: Four surrounding boxes: Encapsulation (wraps data+methods), Inheritance (extends classes), Polymorphism (multiple forms), Abstraction (hides complexity)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing OOP core concepts and their interconnections

diagrams.title: Inheritance Hierarchy Example
diagrams.description: Family-tree style diagram showing inheritance relationships in a vehicle system
elements: Derived: Car (numDoors, honk()), Bike (numGears, pedal()), Truck (cargoCapacity, load())
Arrows pointing from derived to base showing inheritance direction
diagrams.purpose: Understanding inheritance and code reuse visually

diagrams.title: Class Structure in C++
diagrams.description: Diagram showing internal structure of a C++ class with access specifiers
elements: Private section: Private data members (data hiding)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding class composition and data hiding
